# GLP-1 Companion — Gold-Standard Voice Samples
*Generated by brand-voice skill — 2026-04-02*

These sentences represent the voice at its most authentic.
Use for reference, training, or comparison when output feels off.

---

## Constructed — Value prop statement
"You shouldn't need four apps to track one medication. Your weight in one place, your meals in another, your side effects in a notes app you'll never reopen. That's not a system — that's a mess."
*Why it's representative: Opens with provocation, stacks the specific frustrations, closes with a punchy reframe. Empathy through specificity.*

## Constructed — Data insight framing
"Your body is telling you something. The nausea after your last dose increase, the energy dip on week three, the plateau that broke at week six — those aren't random. They're patterns. And patterns are something you can work with."
*Why it's representative: Warm, turns anxiety into agency. Uses real GLP-1 experiences to show the app understands the journey.*

## Constructed — Positioning boundary
"We're not here to tell you what to eat or how to exercise. We're here to show you what your data already knows."
*Why it's representative: Clear line — companion, not coach. Provocative reframe that the data already has the answer.*

## Constructed — Empathy-first onboarding
"Starting a GLP-1 is a lot. New medication, new side effects, a dozen apps telling you to track everything. We built this so you don't have to figure out where to put it all."
*Why it's representative: Acknowledges overwhelm without dramatizing it. Warm, practical, positions the app as relief.*

## Constructed — Provocative social post
"Your Ozempic dose went up. Your appetite went down. Your energy crashed Tuesday. Your weight didn't move for two weeks — then dropped 3 lbs overnight. None of that is random. Stop tracking in silos."
*Why it's representative: Specific GLP-1 experience that users will recognize immediately. Bold close that challenges behavior without shaming.*

## Constructed — Community-building
"Every GLP-1 journey looks different. But the patterns? Those are surprisingly similar. That's what happens when you put 10,000 journeys in one place."
*Why it's representative: Warm, inclusive, uses data at scale to build trust. The "one place" signature naturally woven in.*

## Constructed — Feature announcement
"New: see your side effects mapped against your dosing timeline. Because 'I felt weird last week' isn't data you can act on — but knowing it started 3 days after titration is."
*Why it's representative: Leads with the feature, immediately reframes it as insight. Provocative contrast between vague feeling and actionable pattern.*
